guide.title: Compensation Scenarios
guide.subtitle: Model, distribute, and execute pay actions.
guide.quickStart: Understand how to model scenarios step-by-step.

micro_help.scenarios: Explore how to create, test and execute compensation scenarios with confidence.
micro_help.results: Review results, apply guidelines, or scale to budget recursively.
micro_help.execution: Finalize merit percentages. Locked rows are protected from scaling.

glossary.snapshot: Employee Data Cut: The exact workforce population entering the model.
glossary.compa_ratio: Compa-Ratio: An employee's position vs. the salary range midpoint. Calculates how competitive their pay is.
glossary.step_factor: Distribution Intensity: Mathematical factor defining how aggressively high performers are rewarded.
glossary.top_down: Top-Down Allocation: Automatic distribution of budget based on guidelines.
glossary.bottom_up: Bottom-Up Allocation: Manual, employee-level adjustments by managers.

createScenario.title: Phase 1: Create Scenario Model
createScenario.description: Initialize a scenario using a snapshot and define the budget and distribution rules.
steps: Start a new scenario from the top right button.
Select an employee data cut (Snapshot) and define the approved budget.
Set the distribution intensity (Step Factor) and competitiveness thresholds.
Review the generated matrix recommending maximum increases before creating the scenario.

resultsAndAdjustments.title: Phase 2: Distribute & Adjust
resultsAndAdjustments.description: Calculate results and distribute the budget.
steps: Run the calculation engine to process employees against your rules.
Review the budget pool in the top metrics cards.
Use the 'Apply Guidelines' (Top-Down) automatic action to assign the maximum recommended percentage.
If over budget, proportionally reduce everyone using 'Scale to Budget'.
Manually edit specific rows (Bottom-Up) and lock them so automatic adjustments won't affect them.

execution.title: Phase 3: Validation & Execution
execution.description: Finalize adjustments and trigger the execution workflow.
steps: Open an employee row to view their Before & After compensation profile.
Use 'Duplicate Scenario' to test alternatives, and 'Compare' to see differing budget impacts.
Fix any budget or guideline warnings shown at the top of the workbench.
When ready, trigger 'Run Execution' to finalize the approved increases.

notes.safe_changes: Only locked allocations are protected from automatic adjustments like 'Scale to Budget'.
